analysis.reasoning: Core message: The EU is dispatching newly approved COVID-19 vaccines to all 27 member states for coordinated EU Vaccination Days on 27–29 December. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – implicitly suggests EU capability but does not reference history or overcoming past obstacles; weak/indirect, not clearly promoted. EU2 (EU is united) – explicitly references all 27 EU countries receiving vaccines and a synchronized vaccination campaign, showcasing coordination and solidarity; promoted. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – highlights a practical EU action delivering vaccines for citizens’ health within the EU, demonstrating timely, coordinated benefit; promoted. EU4 (avant-garde) – no claim of pioneering leadership or regulatory firsts; not promoted. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – no human rights/democracy framing; not promoted. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – action is internal to the EU, not global; not promoted. Conclusion: EU2 and EU3 apply.
